case_title: FIRST RESOLUTION INVESTMENT CORPORATION VS. ALAN J DANIELS et al

docket_entries.description: DISCOVERY 612, NOTICE AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES; DECLARE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS - NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF FIRST RESOLUTION INVESTMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES, TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $290, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS)

calendar.matters:
SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing. Comm: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Andrew A. Ford, Esq. appeared for plaintiff First Resolution Investment Corporation.
No appearance by defendant Alan J. Daniels.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on October 2, 2009.
Declaration In Lieu of Live Testimony was filed today, January 19, 2010.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, January 19, 2010.
Plaintiff's Notice to Attend Trial was filed today, January 19, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff First Resolution Investment Corporation, and against defendant Alan J. Daniels as follows:

Principal - $7,781.97.
Interest - $763.27.
Attorney's Fees - $800.00.
Costs - $305.00.
Sanctions Issued November 17, 2010 - $250.00.

Total - $9,900.24.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

Notice And Motion To Compel Responses; Declare Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff First Resolution Investment Corporation's Motion To Compel Responses, To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's Request For Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $290, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2009 Set For Trial On Jan-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
